center.name: Brahmagiri (Karnataka)
center.summary: Brahmagiri is a mountain range in the Western Ghats of south India.

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is found in the higher hills (mostly above 1200 m) of the Nilgiris, Palni Hills, Anaimalai Hills, the Brahmagiri, Bababudan Hills, and Biligiriranga Hills.{{cite book|url= https://archive.org/stream/birdsofindiabein01jerd#page/463/mode/1up|pages=464–465|author=Jerdon TC|title=The Birds of India

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the past the species was considered to have two subspecies, the nominate form in the Nilgiris (earlier called the black-chinned laughingthrush or rufous-breasted laughingthrush) and jerdoni (which is now treated as a full species, the Banasura laughingthrush) with a grey upper breast and found in the Brahmagiris of Coorg and Banasura range of Wayanad
